# TanStack Router

TanStack Router is a modern, type-safe routing library designed for React, Solid, and Vue applications. It provides end-to-end type safety for routes, params, and loaders, with built-in support for schema-driven search params with validation, caching, prefetching, invalidation, nested layouts, transitions, and error boundaries. The router supports both file-based and code-based routing approaches, with file-based routing being the recommended method for automatic code-splitting and superior type inference.

TanStack Start extends TanStack Router into a full-stack framework with server-side rendering (SSR), streaming, and server functions. It enables developers to build production-ready applications with features like server functions for RPC-style server communication, middleware support, static prerendering, and deployment-ready bundling. Both packages work together to provide a comprehensive solution for building modern web applications with excellent developer experience and performance.

## Creating a Router

The `createRouter` function creates a router instance that manages the route tree, matches routes, and coordinates navigations. It's the central configuration point for router-wide settings.

```tsx
import { createRouter, RouterProvider } from '@tanstack/react-router'
import { routeTree } from './routeTree.gen'

const router = createRouter({
  routeTree,
  defaultPreload: 'intent',
  defaultPreloadDelay: 50,
  defaultStaleTime: 0,
  defaultGcTime: 1000 * 60 * 30, // 30 minutes
  scrollRestoration: true,
})

// Register router type for type safety
declare module '@tanstack/react-router' {
  interface Register {
    router: typeof router
  }
}

// In your app
function App() {
  return <RouterProvider router={router} />
}
```

## File-Based Route Definition with createFileRoute

The `createFileRoute` function defines routes in file-based routing. Each file in the routes directory corresponds to a route path, and the function provides full type inference for the route configuration.

```tsx
// src/routes/posts.$postId.tsx
import { createFileRoute } from '@tanstack/react-router'

export const Route = createFileRoute('/posts/$postId')({
  // Validate and parse search params
  validateSearch: (search) => ({
    page: Number(search?.page ?? 1),
    filter: String(search?.filter ?? ''),
  }),

  // Define loader dependencies from search params
  loaderDeps: ({ search }) => ({ page: search.page }),

  // Load data with full type safety
  loader: async ({ params, deps, abortController }) => {
    const response = await fetch(`/api/posts/${params.postId}?page=${deps.page}`, {
      signal: abortController.signal,
    })
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to fetch post')
    return response.json()
  },

  // Pending component shown while loading
  pendingComponent: () => <div>Loading post...</div>,

  // Error component for failed loads
  errorComponent: ({ error }) => <div>Error: {error.message}</div>,

  // Main component
  component: function PostComponent() {
    const post = Route.useLoaderData()
    const { postId } = Route.useParams()
    const { page } = Route.useSearch()

    return (
      <div>
        <h1>{post.title}</h1>
        <p>Post ID: {postId}, Page: {page}</p>
      </div>
    )
  },
})
```

## Root Route with createRootRoute

The root route is the top-most route that encapsulates all other routes. It defines the document shell and global layout, and is always rendered regardless of the current path.

```tsx
// src/routes/__root.tsx
import {
  createRootRoute,
  Outlet,
  Link,
  HeadContent,
  Scripts,
} from '@tanstack/react-router'

export const Route = createRootRoute({
  head: () => ({
    meta: [
      { charSet: 'utf-8' },
      { name: 'viewport', content: 'width=device-width, initial-scale=1' },
      { title: 'My Application' },
    ],
    links: [{ rel: 'stylesheet', href: '/styles.css' }],
  }),

  notFoundComponent: () => <div>404 - Page Not Found</div>,

  component: function RootComponent() {
    return (
      <html>
        <head>
          <HeadContent />
        </head>
        <body>
          <nav>
            <Link to="/" activeProps={{ className: 'active' }}>Home</Link>
            <Link to="/posts" activeProps={{ className: 'active' }}>Posts</Link>
          </nav>
          <main>
            <Outlet />
          </main>
          <Scripts />
        </body>
      </html>
    )
  },
})
```

## Router Context with createRootRouteWithContext

Router context enables dependency injection across all routes. Define a typed context at the root and access it in any route's loader or beforeLoad function.

```tsx
// src/routes/__root.tsx
import { createRootRouteWithContext, Outlet } from '@tanstack/react-router'
import type { QueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

interface RouterContext {
  queryClient: QueryClient
  auth: { user: User | null; isAuthenticated: boolean }
}

export const Route = createRootRouteWithContext<RouterContext>()({
  component: () => <Outlet />,
})

// src/router.tsx
import { createRouter } from '@tanstack/react-router'
import { QueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'
import { routeTree } from './routeTree.gen'

const queryClient = new QueryClient()

export const router = createRouter({
  routeTree,
  context: {
    queryClient,
    auth: undefined!, // Will be provided by RouterProvider
  },
})

// src/App.tsx
import { RouterProvider } from '@tanstack/react-router'
import { useAuth } from './auth'
import { router } from './router'

function App() {
  const auth = useAuth()
  return <RouterProvider router={router} context={{ auth }} />
}
```

## Link Component for Navigation

The Link component renders type-safe anchor tags with support for dynamic params, search params, preloading, and active state styling.

```tsx
import { Link } from '@tanstack/react-router'

function Navigation() {
  return (
    <nav>
      {/* Basic link */}
      <Link to="/">Home</Link>

      {/* Link with path params */}
      <Link to="/posts/$postId" params={{ postId: '123' }}>
        View Post
      </Link>

      {/* Link with search params */}
      <Link to="/posts" search={{ page: 2, sort: 'newest' }}>
        Posts Page 2
      </Link>

      {/* Update search params functionally */}
      <Link to="." search={(prev) => ({ ...prev, page: prev.page + 1 })}>
        Next Page
      </Link>

      {/* Preload on hover with active styling */}
      <Link
        to="/posts/$postId"
        params={{ postId: '456' }}
        preload="intent"
        preloadDelay={100}
        activeProps={{ style: { fontWeight: 'bold', color: 'blue' } }}
        inactiveProps={{ style: { color: 'gray' } }}
      >
        Featured Post
      </Link>

      {/* Link with hash */}
      <Link to="/docs" hash="installation">
        Installation Guide
      </Link>
    </nav>
  )
}
```

## useNavigate Hook for Programmatic Navigation

The useNavigate hook provides imperative navigation for use in event handlers and side effects when Link components aren't suitable.

```tsx
import { useNavigate } from '@tanstack/react-router'

function PostEditor() {
  const navigate = useNavigate()

  const handleSubmit = async (formData: FormData) => {
    try {
      const response = await fetch('/api/posts', {
        method: 'POST',
        body: formData,
      })
      const { id } = await response.json()

      // Navigate to the new post
      navigate({
        to: '/posts/$postId',
        params: { postId: id },
        replace: true, // Replace current history entry
      })
    } catch (error) {
      navigate({
        to: '/error',
        search: { message: 'Failed to create post' },
      })
    }
  }

  const handleCancel = () => {
    // Navigate back
    navigate({ to: '..' })
  }

  return (
    <form onSubmit={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); handleSubmit(new FormData(e.currentTarget)) }}>
      <input name="title" placeholder="Title" />
      <textarea name="content" placeholder="Content" />
      <button type="submit">Create</button>
      <button type="button" onClick={handleCancel}>Cancel</button>
    </form>
  )
}
```

## Search Params with Validation

TanStack Router provides JSON-first search params with schema validation using libraries like Zod, Valibot, or ArkType.

```tsx
// src/routes/shop.products.tsx
import { createFileRoute } from '@tanstack/react-router'
import { zodValidator, fallback } from '@tanstack/zod-adapter'
import { z } from 'zod'

const productSearchSchema = z.object({
  page: fallback(z.number(), 1).default(1),
  limit: fallback(z.number(), 20).default(20),
  category: fallback(z.string(), '').default(''),
  sort: fallback(z.enum(['price', 'name', 'newest']), 'newest').default('newest'),
  inStock: fallback(z.boolean(), false).default(false),
})

export const Route = createFileRoute('/shop/products')({
  validateSearch: zodValidator(productSearchSchema),

  loaderDeps: ({ search }) => ({
    page: search.page,
    limit: search.limit,
    category: search.category,
    sort: search.sort,
  }),

  loader: async ({ deps }) => {
    const params = new URLSearchParams({
      page: String(deps.page),
      limit: String(deps.limit),
      category: deps.category,
      sort: deps.sort,
    })
    const response = await fetch(`/api/products?${params}`)
    return response.json()
  },

  component: function ProductList() {
    const products = Route.useLoaderData()
    const search = Route.useSearch()
    const navigate = Route.useNavigate()

    return (
      <div>
        <select
          value={search.sort}
          onChange={(e) => navigate({ search: (prev) => ({ ...prev, sort: e.target.value }) })}
        >
          <option value="newest">Newest</option>
          <option value="price">Price</option>
          <option value="name">Name</option>
        </select>

        {products.map((product) => (
          <div key={product.id}>{product.name}</div>
        ))}

        <button
          onClick={() => navigate({ search: (prev) => ({ ...prev, page: prev.page + 1 }) })}
        >
          Next Page
        </button>
      </div>
    )
  },
})
```

## Authentication with beforeLoad

The beforeLoad function runs before route loading and is ideal for authentication checks, redirects, and injecting route-specific context.

```tsx
// src/routes/_authenticated.tsx
import { createFileRoute, redirect, isRedirect } from '@tanstack/react-router'

export const Route = createFileRoute('/_authenticated')({
  beforeLoad: async ({ context, location }) => {
    try {
      if (!context.auth.isAuthenticated) {
        throw redirect({
          to: '/login',
          search: { redirect: location.href },
        })
      }

      // Optionally verify session
      const user = await context.auth.verifySession()
      return { user }
    } catch (error) {
      if (isRedirect(error)) throw error

      // Auth check failed, redirect to login
      throw redirect({
        to: '/login',
        search: { redirect: location.href },
      })
    }
  },
})

// src/routes/_authenticated/dashboard.tsx
import { createFileRoute } from '@tanstack/react-router'

export const Route = createFileRoute('/_authenticated/dashboard')({
  component: function Dashboard() {
    const { user } = Route.useRouteContext()
    return <div>Welcome, {user.name}!</div>
  },
})
```

## useSearch and useParams Hooks

Access validated search params and path params with full type safety using route-specific hooks.

```tsx
import { createFileRoute, getRouteApi } from '@tanstack/react-router'

export const Route = createFileRoute('/users/$userId/posts/$postId')({
  validateSearch: (search) => ({
    tab: (search?.tab as string) || 'content',
    highlight: Boolean(search?.highlight),
  }),

  component: function UserPost() {
    // Access via Route object
    const { userId, postId } = Route.useParams()
    const { tab, highlight } = Route.useSearch()

    return (
      <div>
        <h1>User {userId} - Post {postId}</h1>
        <p>Current tab: {tab}</p>
      </div>
    )
  },
})

// Access from deeply nested components using getRouteApi
function PostTabs() {
  const routeApi = getRouteApi('/users/$userId/posts/$postId')
  const params = routeApi.useParams()
  const search = routeApi.useSearch()
  const navigate = routeApi.useNavigate()

  return (
    <div>
      <button onClick={() => navigate({ search: { tab: 'content' } })}>
        Content
      </button>
      <button onClick={() => navigate({ search: { tab: 'comments' } })}>
        Comments
      </button>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## useLoaderData Hook

Access data returned from route loaders with automatic type inference.

```tsx
// src/routes/posts.tsx
import { createFileRoute } from '@tanstack/react-router'

interface Post {
  id: string
  title: string
  content: string
  author: { name: string }
}

export const Route = createFileRoute('/posts')({
  loader: async (): Promise<Post[]> => {
    const response = await fetch('/api/posts')
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to fetch posts')
    return response.json()
  },

  component: function Posts() {
    // posts is typed as Post[]
    const posts = Route.useLoaderData()

    return (
      <ul>
        {posts.map((post) => (
          <li key={post.id}>
            <Link to="/posts/$postId" params={{ postId: post.id }}>
              {post.title} by {post.author.name}
            </Link>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    )
  },
})
```

## useBlocker Hook for Navigation Blocking

Block navigation when users have unsaved changes with a confirmation dialog.

```tsx
import { useBlocker } from '@tanstack/react-router'
import { useState } from 'react'

function EditForm() {
  const [isDirty, setIsDirty] = useState(false)
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ title: '', content: '' })

  const { proceed, reset, status } = useBlocker({
    shouldBlockFn: () => isDirty,
  })

  return (
    <div>
      <form>
        <input
          value={formData.title}
          onChange={(e) => {
            setFormData((prev) => ({ ...prev, title: e.target.value }))
            setIsDirty(true)
          }}
        />
        <textarea
          value={formData.content}
          onChange={(e) => {
            setFormData((prev) => ({ ...prev, content: e.target.value }))
            setIsDirty(true)
          }}
        />
      </form>

      {status === 'blocked' && (
        <dialog open>
          <p>You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave?</p>
          <button onClick={proceed}>Leave</button>
          <button onClick={reset}>Stay</button>
        </dialog>
      )}
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Server Functions with createServerFn (TanStack Start)

Server functions enable type-safe RPC calls from client to server with automatic request handling.

```tsx
// src/utils/posts.functions.ts
import { createServerFn } from '@tanstack/react-start'
import { z } from 'zod'
import { db } from '~/db'

// GET request - fetch posts
export const getPosts = createServerFn({ method: 'GET' })
  .inputValidator(z.object({
    page: z.number().default(1),
    limit: z.number().default(10),
  }))
  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
    const posts = await db.query.posts.findMany({
      limit: data.limit,
      offset: (data.page - 1) * data.limit,
    })
    return posts
  })

// POST request - create post
export const createPost = createServerFn({ method: 'POST' })
  .inputValidator(z.object({
    title: z.string().min(1),
    content: z.string().min(10),
  }))
  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
    const post = await db.insert(posts).values(data).returning()
    return post[0]
  })

// With redirect on error
export const getProtectedData = createServerFn({ method: 'GET' }).handler(async () => {
  const session = await getSession()
  if (!session) {
    throw redirect({ to: '/login' })
  }
  return fetchProtectedData(session.userId)
})

// Usage in components
import { useServerFn } from '@tanstack/react-start'
import { getPosts, createPost } from '~/utils/posts.functions'

function PostsPage() {
  const getPostsFn = useServerFn(getPosts)
  const createPostFn = useServerFn(createPost)

  const { data: posts } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['posts'],
    queryFn: () => getPostsFn({ data: { page: 1, limit: 10 } }),
  })

  const handleCreate = async (formData: FormData) => {
    await createPostFn({
      data: {
        title: formData.get('title') as string,
        content: formData.get('content') as string,
      },
    })
  }

  return <div>{/* ... */}</div>
}
```

## TanStack Start Root Route Setup

Configure the root route for TanStack Start with SSR support, including the document shell with HeadContent and Scripts components.

```tsx
// src/routes/__root.tsx
import {
  Outlet,
  createRootRoute,
  HeadContent,
  Scripts,
} from '@tanstack/react-router'
import type { ReactNode } from 'react'

export const Route = createRootRoute({
  head: () => ({
    meta: [
      { charSet: 'utf-8' },
      { name: 'viewport', content: 'width=device-width, initial-scale=1' },
      { title: 'My TanStack Start App' },
    ],
    links: [
      { rel: 'icon', href: '/favicon.ico' },
      { rel: 'stylesheet', href: '/styles.css' },
    ],
    scripts: [
      { src: '/analytics.js', async: true },
    ],
  }),
  component: RootComponent,
  notFoundComponent: () => <div>404 - Not Found</div>,
})

function RootComponent() {
  return (
    <RootDocument>
      <Outlet />
    </RootDocument>
  )
}

function RootDocument({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en">
      <head>
        <HeadContent />
      </head>
      <body>
        <header>
          <nav>{/* Navigation */}</nav>
        </header>
        <main>{children}</main>
        <Scripts />
      </body>
    </html>
  )
}
```

## Router Configuration with Vite Plugin

Configure TanStack Router with the Vite plugin for file-based routing and automatic route generation.

```tsx
// vite.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import { TanStackRouterVite } from '@tanstack/router-plugin/vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    TanStackRouterVite({
      // Route generation options
      routesDirectory: './src/routes',
      generatedRouteTree: './src/routeTree.gen.ts',
      routeFileIgnorePattern: '.(test|spec).(ts|tsx)$',

      // Code splitting options
      autoCodeSplitting: true,
    }),
    react(),
  ],
})

// For TanStack Start
import { tanstackStart } from '@tanstack/react-start/plugin/vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    tanstackStart({
      // Start-specific options
      serverFns: {
        generateFunctionId: ({ filename, functionName }) => {
          return `${filename}--${functionName}`
        },
      },
    }),
    react(),
  ],
})
```

## Deferred Data Loading with defer

Use defer to stream non-critical data to the client while showing the main content immediately.

```tsx
import { createFileRoute, defer, Await } from '@tanstack/react-router'
import { Suspense } from 'react'

export const Route = createFileRoute('/posts/$postId')({
  loader: async ({ params }) => {
    // Critical data - awaited immediately
    const post = await fetchPost(params.postId)

    // Non-critical data - deferred
    const commentsPromise = fetchComments(params.postId)
    const relatedPostsPromise = fetchRelatedPosts(params.postId)

    return {
      post,
      comments: defer(commentsPromise),
      relatedPosts: defer(relatedPostsPromise),
    }
  },

  component: function PostPage() {
    const { post, comments, relatedPosts } = Route.useLoaderData()

    return (
      <article>
        <h1>{post.title}</h1>
        <p>{post.content}</p>

        <Suspense fallback={<div>Loading comments...</div>}>
          <Await promise={comments}>
            {(data) => (
              <section>
                <h2>Comments</h2>
                {data.map((comment) => (
                  <div key={comment.id}>{comment.text}</div>
                ))}
              </section>
            )}
          </Await>
        </Suspense>

        <Suspense fallback={<div>Loading related posts...</div>}>
          <Await promise={relatedPosts}>
            {(data) => (
              <aside>
                <h2>Related Posts</h2>
                {data.map((related) => (
                  <Link key={related.id} to="/posts/$postId" params={{ postId: related.id }}>
                    {related.title}
                  </Link>
                ))}
              </aside>
            )}
          </Await>
        </Suspense>
      </article>
    )
  },
})
```

## Search Middleware for Persistent Params

Use search middlewares to retain or strip search params across navigations.

```tsx
import { createFileRoute, retainSearchParams, stripSearchParams } from '@tanstack/react-router'
import { zodValidator } from '@tanstack/zod-adapter'
import { z } from 'zod'

// Retain specific search params across all child route navigations
const searchSchema = z.object({
  locale: z.string().default('en'),
  theme: z.enum(['light', 'dark']).default('light'),
  debug: z.boolean().default(false),
})

export const Route = createRootRoute({
  validateSearch: zodValidator(searchSchema),
  search: {
    middlewares: [
      // Keep locale and theme in URL across navigations
      retainSearchParams(['locale', 'theme']),
      // Strip debug param when it's the default value
      stripSearchParams({ debug: false }),
    ],
  },
})

// Usage in child routes - locale and theme are automatically retained
function ChildComponent() {
  return (
    <Link to="/other-page">
      {/* locale and theme will be preserved in the URL */}
      Go to Other Page
    </Link>
  )
}
```

## Summary

TanStack Router and TanStack Start provide a comprehensive solution for building modern web applications with a focus on type safety, developer experience, and performance. The core use cases include: building single-page applications with complex routing requirements, creating full-stack applications with SSR and server functions, implementing type-safe navigation with validated search params, managing authentication flows with route guards, and optimizing performance through automatic code-splitting and data preloading.

Integration patterns include: using file-based routing for automatic route generation and code-splitting, integrating with TanStack Query for advanced data caching and mutations, leveraging router context for dependency injection of services and external libraries, implementing search param validation with Zod or other schema libraries, and deploying TanStack Start applications to various hosting platforms including Vercel, Netlify, and Cloudflare. The library's strong TypeScript support ensures that route definitions, navigation, and data loading are all fully type-checked, catching errors at compile time rather than runtime.
